message:
test(runtime): add coverage for waker drain cycle, yield_now poll sequence, and task ID canonicalization

Adds three new unit tests and one documentation improvement across the
runtime subsystem to close coverage gaps identified during the scheduler
hardening work:

- waker.rs: wake_after_drain_requeues_task — verifies that a waker can
  re-queue a task after the wake set has been drained, ensuring the
  drain operation properly resets the dedup state rather than permanently
  suppressing subsequent wakes for the same TaskId

- yield_now.rs: yield_now_pending_then_ready_with_single_wake — verifies
  the exact two-poll contract of yield_now(): first poll returns Pending
  and issues exactly one wake, second poll returns Ready with no
  additional wakes. Uses a WakeCounter to assert wake counts precisely.

- task_table.rs: insert_task_canonicalizes_record_id — verifies that
  TaskTable::insert_task() overwrites the TaskRecord's id field with the
  canonical TaskId derived from the actual arena index, even when the
  record was constructed with a stale or incorrect id. This is the
  invariant that the intrusive heap hardening relies on.

- local_queue.rs: adds a clarifying comment explaining why the queue
  lock is dropped before returning from the arena closure in Stealer,
  to minimize lock hold time on contention-heavy steal probes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
